name: Butyrate recovered histone acylation and extended mitochondrial-deficiency mouse lifespan
summary: A 2026 disease-model result adds recent mammalian metabolite-restoration context but is not a normal-aging or human trial signal.
statement: In inducible TFAM-deficient mice, tributyrin/butyrate supplementation delayed multiple signs of multimorbidity, extended lifespan, and recovered intestinal epigenetic histone acylation marks lost with mitochondrial deficiency.
population_or_model: Inducible TFAM-deficient mice with premature mitochondrial-dysfunction multimorbidity
time_horizon: Post-induction disease course and survival follow-up
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract reports lifespan extension but does not provide a percentage effect size.
caveats: This is a severe mitochondrial-deficiency disease model, not normal aging.
The intervention is also microbiome/metabolite and mitochondrial-context biology, not isolated chromatin modulation.
No human mitochondrial-disease or aging trial result is established here.
